meaning: n.戏法,骗术,诡辩,巧妙的花招
examples: Some accused the White House of legerdemain.
Usually, they are the result of statistical legerdemain.
But this week's budget "surprise" cannot be put down simply to financial legerdemain.
Were it not for this bit of accounting legerdemain, the true Debt to GDP ratio would show up as a ratio of 119 percent.
This legerdemain reflects especially badly on the slick Mr Kitao.
